title: CAREER | Blue Education
screenshot_note: Screenshot captured with ID ss_8905rolvo - requires manual download from browser
main_content:
CAREER SERVICES

What are the challenges do you typically face when looking for work in the Australian job marketplace?
- It is difficult or almost impossible to find a full time job in Australia for an international student graduate.
- I don't even get a reply for the CVs I have submitted!
- How many applications do I have to send?
- Most companies want to hire people with experience!

Our Career Counsellors are here to help graduates find their niche in the job marketplace. We also help new migrants, graduates or current job seekers who are looking at career options, or even considering a career change and thus requiring advice in planning and execution.

We can guide you through any job application process, get acquainted with cultural nuances in the business world, and consider some winning strategies to stay ahead when submitting your job applications. We can help you design an appealing resume, carry out personal coaching for interview sessions or even help polish your business communication skills.

Services include:
- Career Counselling
- Resume Writing Services
- Interview Coaching
- Job Search Strategies
- Cultural Adaptation Initiatives
- Personality and Career Assessment
- Communication Skills Training

We work with professional 3rd parties who can provide that additional support for our clients. You can find out more about our Employability Booster Programme or speak to our Counsellor if you are eligible for this programme. We take pride in working with Employment Advantage in getting our international student graduates 'ready' for work.

title: EMPLOYABILITY BOOSTER PROGRAMME | Blue Education
screenshot_note: Screenshot captured with ID ss_1441c09t7 - requires manual download from browser
main_content:
EMPLOYABILITY BOOSTER PROGRAMME

Our Employability Booster Programme (EBP) is a 6-phase approach. We will assist in the initial phases of 'Assessment' and 'Readiness' and you should be able to position yourself better in the job marketplace. However, if you require for us to connect you with potential employers or relevant host companies, we are able to guide you through in the rest of the programme from 'Profiling' all the way to 'Review'. Below provides an overview of our assistance in the life-cycle of the EBP.

01 ASSESSMENT
Review of candidate's requirements, background and job readiness.
- Initial interview to ascertain candidate's strengths and areas for improvement.
- Candidate to complete Initial Placement Questionnaire (IPQ)
- Candidate to complete Online Readiness Training.

02 READINESS
Ascertain candidate's inclination towards being 'ready' for employment.
- Assist with the preparation of a professional CV.
- Provide additional support i.e. presence in LinkedIn, tips on presentation and image, and understand of cultural context.

03 PROFILING
Assess candidate's requirements and match with potential employers.
- Research on shortlisted employers.
- Develop letters/templates of approach.
- Candidate to contact prospective employers.

04 PREPARATION
Get candidate ready and positioned for interviews in the job marketplace.
- Work with candidate to develop a target list of potential employers.
- Shortlist and prioritise employers to approach.

05 PLACEMENT
Facilitate opportunities for internship and interviews.
- Assist in lining up interviews and internship.
- Assist candidate to prepare for interviews, provide feedback and action plans.

06 REVIEW
Gain feedback on candidate's performance.
- Contact employer, or the companies who have interviewed or hired candidate to obtain feedback.
- Provide feedback to candidate.

If you would like to participate in our Employability Booster Programme, prepare your CV and send to us. Our team will assess your eligibility and will notify you for an appointment with our Career Counsellor.

title: EDUCATION | Blue Education
screenshot_note: Screenshot captured with ID ss_75622kfpf - requires manual download from browser
main_content:
EDUCATION SERVICES

Our education counsellors services include assessing of student future aspiration and recommendations of pathways, courses and institute in Australia. We are here to help you to gain admission and assist you through the entire school and visa application process.

From primary school to university studies, we have assisted thousands of clients from 40 different countries in the past 20 years.

If you are considering a short-term study abroad experience, such as an English language program or even thinking of extending your study in Australia. Curious about your study option, we are here to help, from primary, secondary to pursuing a full degree program such as study abroad, bachelors, Graduate or PhD.

SERVICES INCLUDES:
- Application for student visa/ renewal or extension of student visa
- Enrolment of course/ change of course
- Change of school /& programme
- Advice on course selection and school visit
- Advice on overall education, migration and career pathway planning

If you would like for us to assist you in any of the above services
If you are ready for us to start your enrolment process, click here

title: EMPLOYER SPONSORED | Blue Education
screenshot_note: Screenshot captured with ID ss_2310394d6 - requires manual download from browser
main_content:
EMPLOYER SPONSORED

There are a few visa options available that allow skilled workers to come to Australia and work for an approved business as their skills and job experiences are in demand in Australia.

It is important to seek our advice to understand the requirements involved and the best working visa applicable to your circumstances. Book your consultation session with one of our experienced registered migration agents to find out more, we can schedule a SKYPE/LINE Consultation session even if you are based offshore.

Employers can sponsor or nominate workers to remain in Australia for both temporary and permanent basis.

Temporary Skill Shortage visa (subclass 482)
This temporary visa lets an employer sponsor a suitably skilled worker to fill a position they can't find a suitably skilled Australian to fill.

Sponsorship obligations
Approved sponsors must meet certain sponsorship obligations. The sponsorship obligations will depend on the type of sponsor.

Permanent Employer Sponsored Programme

Employer Nomination Scheme visa (subclass 186)
Employers in Australia can sponsor skilled workers to remain in Australia on a permanent basis through the Employer Nomination Scheme.

Regional Sponsored Migration Scheme visa (subclass 187)
This visa will allow a skilled worker to remain in Australia on a permanent basis and work in a regional area (this visa is closed for new applicants).

Skilled Employer Sponsored Regional (Provisional) visa (subclass 494)
This visa enables regional employers to address identified labour shortages within their region by sponsoring skilled workers where employers can't source an appropriately skilled Australian worker.

There are other pathways in which you may consider if the above options do not suit you. Ensure that you book your consultation session and speak with our Licensed Migration Agent.

title: ENGLISH COURSES | Blue Education
screenshot_note: Screenshot captured with ID ss_8298ufagr - requires manual download from browser
main_content:
ENGLISH COURSES

"Take advantage of every opportunity to practice your communication skills so that when important occasions arise, you will have the gift, the style, the sharpness, the clarity, and the emotions to affect other people." - Jim Rohn

By studying English in Australia, you are compelled to speak and engage others around you in their native language, English. There is no better way to immerse in the beauty, culture and language of your host country so that you may take this experience with you that will change your life forever.

General English
Focusing on communication skills and understand of English language rules, structure, vocabulary and grammar

English for Academic Purposes
Preparing you for study at an English-speaking university, higher education or vocational institute. Courses are offered at private English colleges and University English colleges and may be recognised by further education institutes, classes are usually offered in 10 weeks, 20 weeks or 30 weeks block

English Language Bridging
Pathway English program offered within English-speaking University. Courses are offered only offered with University English colleges and study period are in blocks of 10 weeks to 20 weeks
* Pre-requisite of English assessment or IELTS / Cambridge or Pearson result required.

English for Specific Purposes (ESP)
Focusing on practical English for specific course areas such as business, aviation or tourism

Examination Preparation (IELTS Preparation / Cambridge English)
Preparing you for English language proficiency examinations. Exam format usually includes testing of 4 different areas including Reading, Writing, Listening and Speaking

Certificate (I - IV) of Spoken and Written English
Provide a general education framework for learners to develop English language and literacy skills to have the ability to speak and write maintain participation in the general community.

English for Teaching
These courses include TESOL and CELTA (Teaching English to Speakers of Other Languages, EfTC (English for Teaching Children)

title: NEWS & EVENTS | Blue Education
screenshot_note: Screenshot captured with ID ss_4927f2k2x - requires manual download from browser
main_content:
NEWS & EVENTS

We welcome you to engage with our team during our biennial event, the Springboard to Australia or our regular 1-on-1 Cuppa Sessions

REGISTRATION FOR FUTURE EVENTS

From time to time, we will hold events and online information sharing sessions on study, migration and career planning in which you can participate. You can register your interest below and we will notify you when our session is scheduled. Below provides some of the usual events we hold as we invite individuals interested in studying, working, or living in Australia to connect with us for a personalised consult to discuss and plan your options.

Study in Australia | 1-on-1 Consultation
Watch out for the dates in 2025!
This is a session dedicated to SPM|STPM |O/A-level leavers. There are hundreds or study programmes to choose from and scholarship offers. Our Counsellor from Australia will be in Malaysia to see students and families on individual counselling sessions.

International Students Get Together
Watch out for the dates in 2025!
We welcome international students who would like to discuss about their concerns or queries about visas or migration opportunities with a Registered Migration Agent.

If you have queries on any of the following, do not miss out this opportunity:
- 189 Skilled Independent visa
- 190 State Sponsored visa
- 489 Skilled Regional visa
- 820 Partner visa
- 500 Student visa
- 600 Visitor visa
- 870 Parent visa
- 485 Temporary Graduate visa
- 407 Training visa

We will have light refreshments for an evening of sharing and you can also discuss about work and career opportunities with our Career Counsellor.

Springboard to Australia
Watch out for the dates in 2026!
Springboard to Australia (STA) is an event that Blue Education initiated in a few major cities in Malaysia, Taiwan and Ghana back in 2016. In the past, STA provided a platform for students to learn about study programmes, schools and career opportunities in Australia. This year, Blue Education and its key partner, GLP Lawyers International, will present to the audience about Australia, study pathway options, student visa requirements, work placement, migration and investment opportunities that would be of interest to many in the target locations.

Join us for an afternoon or evening session if you are interested to find out more about:
- High school, foundation, diploma, degree or post graduate degree programmes.
- Study and work placement strategies.
- Scholarship opportunities.
- Study & Skilled Migration pathways.
- Investment & Talent Migration.
